message:
perf(patterns): switch Classification strings to Cow<'static, str> for zero-alloc hot paths

Convert the Classification, ClassificationTier, and ClassificationDetails
structs from owned String fields to Cow<'static, str> for reason and
tier-name fields. This eliminates thousands of small heap allocations on
the command classification hot path:

- Classification.reason: String → Cow<'static, str>
- ClassificationTier.name: String → Cow<'static, str>
- ClassificationTier.reason: String → Cow<'static, str>

All constructor methods now accept impl Into<Cow<'static, str>>, so
callers passing string literals get zero-cost &'static str references
while dynamic strings still work via Cow::Owned.

Add tier name constants (TIER_INSTANT_REJECT, TIER_STRUCTURE_ANALYSIS,
TIER_KEYWORD_FILTER, TIER_NEVER_INTERCEPT, TIER_FULL_CLASSIFICATION) to
avoid repeated string construction per classification call.

Update lib.rs re-exports, security tests, and proptest harness to match
the new Cow-based API signatures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
